Data.text:
`AttachMode = ByInstance` in the API (`NAppAttachMode.ByInstance`) means:

- Further UIA APIs will search **only in the indicated application instance**, including all parent/child windows (alerts, popups, etc.).
- Other instances of the same app/browser are **excluded**.  
[Uipath Uiautomationnext Api Contracts Iuiautomationappservice Attach](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/activities/other/latest/ui-automation/uipath-uiautomationnext-api-contracts-iuiautomationappservice-attach)

If it “attaches to the wrong page”, typical causes (based on docs behavior):

- The instance you attached to already has a different tab active.
- Your later actions are not constrained to a specific tab element.

Regarding navigation:

- To change the page in the attached browser/tab, you should use a navigation activity such as **[Navigate Browser](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/activities/other/latest/ui-automation/n-navigate-browser)** inside **[Use Application/Browser](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/activities/other/latest/ui-automation/n-application-card)**.
- `Navigate Browser` supports **Go Back / Go Forward / Refresh / Close Tab** (and **Go Home** in Windows‑Legacy).

So yes: if the attached instance is not already on the desired page, you need a navigation step (e.g. `Navigate Browser` or opening the correct URL in `Use Application/Browser`) to reach the correct page/tab.

---
followups: How can I ensure Attach with ByInstance always targets a specific browser tab?
